mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hike months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below zero across all test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained economic expansion, a month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of expansion-phase INDPRO increases, or the mechanism yields a negative cumulative alpha over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the consumer price index accelerates inflation expectations, prompting immediate equity risk premium repricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 12-month rolling window, or CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of occurrences, or interaction term coefficients are statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 1.5 percent, aggregate demand weakness triggers earnings downgrades and defensive portfolio rotation, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 readings below 1.5 percent fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a win rate above 45 percent, or the signal generates a positive cumulative return in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflation regime characterized by rising inflation and falling industrial output, supply-side constraints compress profit margins and force monetary tightening, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The stagflation signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered cases, or the joint indicator fails to achieve a statistically significant negative coefficient in out-of-sample models.
